key_insight: A pretrained image-editing model can act as a general numerical solver when physical states and parameters are encoded as images and lightweight adapters.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a single vision-based framework could prototype or approximate many physics problems—from diffusion and fluids to elasticity and fracture—using the same training recipe.
method: Inject scalar parameters through lightweight adapters.
Train on supervision from established numerical or analytic solvers across many PDEs and physics tasks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful modeling of heterogeneous static and time-dependent systems, including unstable and shock-like behavior, while also noting a failed Kuramoto-Sivashinsky long-horizon case.
watch_item: Image/latent representations make it harder to control numerical range and enforce governing laws or invariants directly, which can limit reliability on chaotic dynamics.
paper_title: Image Editing Models are Numerical Solvers
